content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Reviewed the iterative_minimal workflow implementation. FlowPreset::IterativeMinimal is correctly wired through domain, config, engine, journal, and CLI layers as an additive change — existing Minimal and other flows remain single-pass. The iterative loop emits ImplementerIterationStarted/Completed/LoopExited journal events, validates settings (positive integers, stable_rounds_required <= max_consecutive_implementer_rounds) with defense-in-depth at both config and engine layers, and honors the cancellation token before emitting a loop-exit event when no iteration was attempted. Git diff fingerprinting combines HEAD SHA with staged+unstaged+untracked content, excludes .ralph-burning and build artifacts via pathspecs, and falls back to a workspace filesystem walk when no git repo exists. Resume recovery for terminal iterations uses persisted parsed-payload sidecars (it{N} suffix prevents collision with primary stage payload), with rewind-and-reinvoke fallback and stale loop-exit event deduplication. Stable exit takes priority over max-rounds exit. Tests cover stability exit, max-rounds exit, sidecar persistence failure, resume with missing sidecar, stale exit-event mismatch, dirty-file change detection, HEAD-only commit detection, no-git filesystem fallback, and that the standard Minimal flow remains unaffected. No fix-now correctness bugs or safety issues identified at this pass.

## Amendments

None.
